subject: Anthropology
title: Adoption Across Cultures
description: A fast breakdown of Adoption Across Cultures for builders.

hook.text: In Oceania, giving a child to relatives is a gift, not a loss. Adoption strengthens kinship bonds, not breaks them.

buildup.text: Adoption is practiced in every culture, but its meaning varies. Some treat it as permanent transfer, others as sharing.

discovery.text: In Polynesian cultures, circulating children among kin builds alliances. Adoption creates ties, not replaces them.

twist.text: Western adoption often hides the birth family. Many cultures keep both families openly connected to the child.

climax.text: Adoption reveals that parenthood is socially defined. Every society decides what makes someone a parent.

punchline.text: Parenthood is defined by the culture, not the genes.

quiz.question: How does adoption function in Polynesian cultures?
options: It severs all ties with birth parents
Circulating children among kin builds alliances and ties
It doesn't exist in Polynesian cultures
